analysis.problem: Political prisoners are being held without fair trial or due process in multiple countries, undermining human rights and dignity.
analysis.root_cause: Authoritarian regimes and corrupt governments use legal systems to suppress dissent, control populations, and maintain power.
leverage_points: International human rights organizations and advocacy groups
Global public opinion and media exposure

proposed_actions.action: Increase funding and support for legal aid organizations working with political prisoners
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce the number of political prisoners by 15% within 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Launch a global campaign to raise awareness about political prisoners and their cases
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Achieve 10 million social media impressions and 500,000 petition signatures within 6 months

evidence: Ruth López, a human rights lawyer in El Salvador, is still in jail one year after her arrest.
Pastor Ezra Jin is being held in China without fair trial or due process.
analysis.generalization: This issue connects to the broader domains of governance, justice, and human rights, highlighting the need for systemic reform and international cooperation.
analysis.summary: Political prisoners are being held without fair trial or due process, undermining human rights and dignity globally.

raw_items.title: El Salvador: Human Rights Lawyer Still in Jail One Year on
raw_items.summary:
Click to expand Image Ruth López. © El Diario de Hoy

(Washington, DC) – The prominent anti-corruption lawyer Ruth López remains in pretrial detention in El Salvador with her case under judicial seal one year after her arrest, Human Rights Watch said today.
